events.note: First TestFlight build (#3, marketing 1.0.0) submitted by Josh + FINISHED 2026-06-27 (interactive first-bind of the EAS-vault ASC key); recorded after the fact so the production gate reflects reality. Confirmed live on ASC: build #3 processingState=VALID, not expired.
iOS build #4 (marketing 1.0.0) built --local + eas submit → TestFlight, FINISHED 2026-06-28. Unattended (key already bound from build #3).
Android cold first release: build --local .aab + eas submit -p android → Play Internal testing, FINISHED 2026-06-28 (submission 23cb23c0). Non-interactive from the first release.
iOS build #6 (new two-people icon) -> TestFlight, FINISHED 2026-06-28.
Android build (new icon) -> Play Internal, FINISHED 2026-06-28 (submission 98b3cd2a). First attempt errored on a gzip-tar artifact; extracted the real .aab + re-submitted; ship-eas.sh hardened.

iOS build #7 (marketing 1.0.0) — on-device UX feedback fixes (robust contacts import, draft-based new-person flow with required name + Save FAB, info/action separation, HTC existing-person picker + keyboard-safe scroll + Save) plus full in-app (domain) translation into es/de/fr/it/pt-BR/ja. Built --local + eas submit -> TestFlight, FINISHED (submission 3b476066). Unattended.

Android (same payload as iOS #7) -> Play Internal, FINISHED (submission c6ed19ba). GOTCHA: raw `eas build -p android --local --output X.aab` wrote a gzip-tar (bundle/release/app-release.aab + apk/debug/app-debug.apk), so the first submit ERRORED 'APKs are not allowed for this application'; extracted the real app-release.aab and re-submitted. Next time use the factory ship script (handles extraction) or extract before `eas submit`.
